prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: 3/5 stars. Waking Up is a dependable, no-frills consciousness delivery service that arrives daily without needing to reorder, though it insists on bundling with Sleep (sold separately). Setup is automatic but requires lying horizontally for 6–8 hours beforehand; documentation is minimal and the onboarding sequence ranges from dim ambient light to a shrieking third-party alarm add-on. Performance is inconsistent: cold mornings and Mondays cause noticeable lag, and the product often boots into an unskippable patch called Grogginess 1.0, which disables coherent speech and fine motor control for 7–23 minutes. Integration with Coffee improves throughput but increases jitter. The Snooze feature works as advertised until it catastrophically fails, at which point the unit launches directly into Meetings. Pros: daylight access, renewed control of limbs, re-entry to To‑Do List. Cons: dream loss on exit, face-crease artifacts, breath malfunctions, existential pop-ups, and random joint notifications. Nonrefundable, noncancelable subscription; skipping a day voids most plans. Pairs best with sunlight; customer support (Circadian) never replies. Adequate if you need consciousness, but not a premium experience. Verified Purchase.
